keyPoints: Inner loop runs i times for each iteration of i (from 1 to n)
Best-case time complexity: Ω(n²) as at least one iteration always occurs

questions.question: Solve the recurrence relation T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n using the Master Theorem and state its time complexity in Big-O notation. Also explain the three cases of the Master Theorem.
keyPoints: Here a=2, b=2, f(n)=n, so n^(log₂2) = n¹
Compare f(n) = n with n^1: they are equal (n^1 * log⁰n = n)
Case 2 of Master Theorem applies: T(n) = Θ(n log n)
Case 2 applies when f(n) = Θ(n^log_b(a) * log^k n) where k=0
Best case: Ω(1) - element found at middle position in first comparison
Worst case: O(log n) - element not present or at extreme ends, halving search space each time
Average case: Θ(log n) - assuming equal probability of element being at any position
Recurrence: T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1), solving gives T(n) = O(log n)

questions.question:
(a) Arrange the following functions in increasing order of asymptotic complexity. Also justify your answer:

f₁(n) = n!, f₂(n) = 2^n, f₃(n) = n³, f₄(n) = n² log n, f₅(n) = n log n, f₆(n) = n

(b) Prove that if T₁(n) = O(f(n)) and T₂(n) = O(g(n)), then T₁(n) + T₂(n) = O(max(f(n), g(n))).

(c) Define and differentiate between time complexity and space complexity with an example each.
keyPoints: Order: n < n log n < n² log n < n³ < 2^n < n! (factorial grows faster than exponential)
For (b): T₁(n) + T₂(n) ≤ c₁f(n) + c₂g(n) ≤ 2C max(f(n), g(n)) = O(max(f(n), g(n)))
Time complexity measures execution time as function of input size; space complexity measures memory usage
Example: Merge Sort has O(n log n) time and O(n) space; Recursive Fibonacci has O(2^n) time and O(n) space
